edibility: Too tough to chew; consumed as tea or extract for medicinal benefits. Contains PSK (polysaccharide-K), a compound studied extensively in cancer therapy and immune support.
taste: Earthy, mildly bitter when brewed as tea.
search: Found year-round on dead hardwood logs and stumps in woodlands; perennial fruiting body persists through seasons. Identify by distinctive concentric rings in bands of white, brown, and blue-green colors, resembling a turkey's tail feathers. Fibonacci-like growth pattern is characteristic. Avoid confusion with false turkey tail (Stereum ostrea) which lacks color variation, and Trichaptum species.
